filesHome.pageSubtitle: Each card is one of your access keys — click to browse the buckets it can see
filesHome.emptyStateDescription: Add a key from your cluster admin to see the buckets it can reach.

adminClustersList.emptyStateDescription: One pane of glass for Garage, MinIO, OpenMaxIO, and AWS S3. Add your first cluster to get started — basement talks to its admin API and surfaces buckets, keys, and layout from here.

adminClustersCreate.pageSubtitle: Register a storage backend so basement can show you buckets, keys, and layout from here.
adminClustersCreate.awsS3Hint: Native AWS S3 with IAM credentials.

adminPolicies.pageSubtitle: Roles, capabilities, and per-user assignments. Edit the matrix to grant or revoke access without re-deploying.
adminPolicies.deprecationBanner: Bucket-level access is now controlled by S3 key assignment on the cluster admin side, not by basement roles. When a user connects a region ( /files/regions/new ), they bring an S3 key that already has the backend's bucket grants attached. basement no longer enforces per-bucket access — the backend does. The bucket_user role remains for backward compatibility but new assignments will have no effect.
adminPolicies.deleteRoleTooltip: Seed roles can be edited but not deleted — prevents accidental lockout.
adminPolicies.deprecationNotice: This role is deprecated. New assignments have no effect — use /files/regions/new instead. Bucket-level access is decided by the backend S3 key attached to the user's UserRegion, not by basement policy. Existing assignments remain valid for back-compat but should be revoked once users have migrated to the region keychain.
adminPolicies.wildcardNotice: This role holds the *: * superuser wildcard. To narrow it, remove the wildcard and pick specific capabilities below.
adminPolicies.simulatorSubtitle: What-if inspector: given a user + capability + scope, why does the enforcer allow or deny? Pure analysis — no policy is changed.

adminServiceAccounts.emptyStateDescription: Create a service account to allow external systems to access buckets with limited permissions.

adminSystem.pageSubtitle: Configure org-level capabilities and feature flags.
adminSystem.oidcOnlyLabel: OIDC Only Mode (hide local password login)
adminSystem.gatewaysDescription: Mount your buckets as a network drive on Mac, Windows, Linux, iOS, and Android. WebDAV is available today and works with any client that speaks the protocol — Finder's Connect to Server, Windows Explorer's Map Network Drive, or your file manager of choice. Additional protocols are placeholders for when implementations are contributed.
adminSystem.gatewayRegistryUnavailable: Gateway registry unavailable: {error}. Check the basement server log; the registry wires up at boot.
adminSystem.windowsConnect: Windows: Explorer › Map network drive › paste mount URL.
adminSystem.linuxConnect: Linux: Nautilus › Connect to Server › replace https:// with davs://.
adminSystem.iosConnect: iOS: Files › Browse › … › Connect to Server.
adminSystem.signInHint: Sign in with basement credentials, or a service-account key ( BMNT… ) for automation.
adminSystem.baseUrlDescription: Set this when basement is behind a reverse proxy on a different host. Leave blank to use the current origin.
adminSystem.sessionTimeoutDescription: How long an elevated admin session stays active before dropping back to user mode. Shorter values are safer; longer values reduce friction for long admin tasks. Operators with an expired session see a banner at the top of admin pages inviting them to re-elevate — their work isn't lost.
adminSystem.rangeDescription: Range: {min}–{max} seconds. Values outside this range are clamped automatically.
adminSystem.oidcMappingsDescription: Declare which OIDC claim values grant which basement roles. Applied on every OIDC login: matching mappings are auto-assigned, stale ones are revoked. Manually-granted roles are never touched. Changes take effect at each user's next sign-in.
adminSystem.noMappingsConfigured: No mappings configured. Without any mappings, OIDC users sign in with no role assignments — you'll need to assign roles manually at /admin/policies.
adminSystem.testMappingHint: Test a mapping by logging the affected user out and back in — existing sessions are unaffected until they re-authenticate.
adminSystem.mappingDescription: When the named claim contains the value, the user is granted the selected role at the given scope on next OIDC sign-in.
adminSystem.scopeGrammarHint: Use the ADR-0001 grammar: host:*, cluster:*, cluster:{cid}, etc.
adminSystem.usageOverviewDescription: Snapshot of storage usage across every cluster: totals, per-cluster breakdown, and the top buckets by size and object count.

adminFirstRun.welcomeSubtitle: One pane of glass for Garage, MinIO, OpenMaxIO, and AWS S3. This quick walkthrough sets up your first cluster and (optionally) single sign-on plus a team invite. You can skip any step.
adminFirstRun.clusterSubtitle: Register a storage backend so basement can show you buckets, keys, and layout from here. Pick a driver and fill in the connection details.
adminFirstRun.oidcSkipMessage: Skip this step if you don't use OIDC — basement's local password login works on its own. You can add OIDC any time from Settings.
adminFirstRun.inviteHint: Generate an invite link to send to your first teammate. You can always add more from /admin/users later.
adminFirstRun.doneSubtitle: Nice. basement is ready to use. Here's where to go next:
adminFirstRun.clustersDoneDescription: Manage your storage backends — buckets, keys, and layout.
adminFirstRun.regionsDoneDescription: The user-side view: browse buckets via your access keys.
adminFirstRun.systemSettingsDoneDescription: Org-wide policies, gateways, OIDC, and session timeouts.
adminFirstRun.gettingStartedDoneDescription: Read the docs to learn more about what basement can do.

authSignup.disabledMessage: Sign-up is currently disabled for this organization. Please contact your administrator.
authSignup.enabledMessage: Sign-up is enabled. Registration functionality will be available soon.

errors.connectionFailed: Couldn't connect to backend. Retrying automatically…
